before_agent_start call (each hook invocation is stateless), so
isBootstrapTurn always returned true and bypassed the bootstrappedSessions
check — causing wake context to be prepended to EVERY user message.

states. bootstrappedSessions Map is now the sole gate:

v2.9: 新增 DS SQL 提取工具

新增 extract_ds_sql.py 脚本，功能：
1. 通过 API 获取指定项目下所有工作流
2. 提取工作流中的 SQL 任务（支持多种 SQL 类型）
3. 自动保存 SQL 到文件（带文件头注释）
4. 生成提取统计报告

使用方法:
  python3 extract_ds_sql.py <project_code> --name <project_name>

示例:
  python3 extract_ds_sql.py 159737550740160 --name okr_ads

测试成功: 从 okr_ads 项目提取了 6 个工作流的 7 个 SQL 任务

existing IPC mechanism: write register_group JSON files to the main
group's IPC tasks directory via ExecSimple.

internally. This reuses the existing group registration flow without
any WeChat-specific changes to NanoClaw's core.

aiohttp.ClientTimeout(total=30) — this fix applies the same
pattern consistently to all platform send functions.

Add MiniMax as a first-class LLM provider (#1662)

MiniMax (https://api.minimax.io) offers an OpenAI-compatible chat
completions API. This commit adds MiniMax to the provider type registry,
authorization header injection, and credential resolution chain so that
operators can route requests to MiniMax models (M2.7, M2.5, etc.)
through the semantic router using the same config-driven workflow as
OpenAI, Anthropic, and other cloud providers.

Replace subprocess transport with HTTP shim for module isolation fix

OpenClaw jiti plugin loader creates isolated module contexts, so
registerApiProvider and wrapStreamFn hooks never reach the main
pi-ai instance. Fix by running a detached HTTP shim process that
translates OpenAI /chat/completions into apple-llm CLI calls.

Security: loopback-only, random bearer token, random port,
auto-shutdown after 10 min idle.

fix: enable automatic container restart on failure or system reboot (#112)

**Current behavior:**
- Docker containers do not restart if the daemon restarts or the machine reboots
- Service downtime until manual intervention

**New behavior:**
- Added 'restart: unless-stopped' policy to postgres and app services
- Containers automatically recover from crashes
- Containers restart after Docker daemon or system reboots
- Exception: containers stopped explicitly via 'docker stop' or 'docker-compose

fix: 支持多种启动模式(gateway start/run)，优先后台模式

- start: 先尝试 `openclaw gateway start`(后台)，失败则 fallback 到 `gateway run`(前台)
- stop: 先尝试 `openclaw gateway stop`，失败则 kill 前台进程
- restart: 先尝试 `openclaw gateway restart`，失败则 stop+start
- logs: 前台模式自动推送，后台模式通过 `openclaw logs --follow`
- 状态检测: 端口探测 + 进程存活检查

fix(core): decouple empty-promise retry budget and compact at 75% context

Two fixes for long-running agent tasks stalling mid-execution:

1. Empty-promise detection now uses its own retry counter instead of
   sharing `scheduled_task_retries` with other nudge mechanisms. This
   prevents incomplete-thought retries from exhausting the budget that
   structural colon-ending detection relies on.

2. Context compaction now triggers at 75% of available tokens (was 100%),
   with memory flush adjuste

fix(tc-review): exit 1 when Claude omits conclusion line (fail closed)

Without this, a "No review findings" response (no tc-review: APPROVED/
NEEDS_CHANGES line) falls through the parser's else-branch and becomes
a false tc_complete(blocked), sending Huahua into an infinite fix loop.

Now harness.sh captures Claude's output, validates the conclusion line
exists, and exits 1 (worker failure) if absent. Menglan routes worker
failures to failed/ rather than producing false NEEDS_CHANGES.

TC-023-0

fix(embedder): use dtype q8 for bge preset to avoid onnx external-data crash

BAAI/bge-m3's fp32 ONNX is split into model.onnx (stub) + model.onnx_data
(2.3 GB), which @huggingface/transformers cannot load.  Setting dtype=q8
selects model_quantized.onnx — a self-contained 570 MB file.

- Add optional dtype field to EmbedderPreset interface
- Pass dtype through HuggingFaceEmbedder to pipeline()
- Set dtype:q8 and update estimatedRAM (~600 MB) on the bge preset
- Add tests asserting bge uses q8 an

- Empty diff → err + exit 1 (PR with no diff still fails closed)
- No head -500 truncation — full diff is passed to Claude unchanged

fix(agent): always prefer streaming for API calls to prevent hung subagents (#3120)

The non-streaming API call path (_interruptible_api_call) had no
wall-clock timeout. When providers keep connections alive with SSE
keep-alive pings but never deliver a response, httpx's inactivity
timeout never fires and the call hangs indefinitely.

Subagents always used the non-streaming path because they have no
stream consumers (quiet_mode=True). This caused delegate_task to
hang for 40+ minutes in producti

fix: handle OpenClaw Gateway mode response format in server.py (V30)

When OpenClaw runs in Gateway mode, the response structure is:
  {"runId": "...", "result": {"payloads": [...], "meta": {...}}}

When in embedded mode, the structure is:
  {"payloads": [...], "meta": {...}}

server.py was only handling embedded mode — in Gateway mode, data.get("payloads")
returns [] and the fallback was str(data), causing raw JSON to be sent to users.

fix(deps): override picomatch to >=4.0.4 to resolve ReDoS CVE

picomatch <4.0.4 has a ReDoS vulnerability via extglob quantifiers
(GHSA-c2c7-rcm5-vvqj). The package is pulled in transitively through
vitest → vite. Upstream vitest/vite have not yet bumped their pinned
range to 4.0.4, so a pnpm.overrides entry forces the patched version
across all dependency paths.

fix(zalo): panic on sync.Map.CompareAndDelete with uncomparable CancelFunc (#491)

runQRFlow stored context.CancelFunc directly in sync.Map and called
CompareAndDelete on cleanup. Function types are not comparable in Go,
causing a runtime panic: "comparing uncomparable type context.CancelFunc".

Wrap CancelFunc in a *cancelEntry struct pointer so sync.Map can compare
by pointer identity. This preserves the original semantics: CompareAndDelete
only removes the entry if it still belongs to the cur

fix: two-step Azure OAuth to support personal Microsoft accounts

Personal MS accounts (gmail, outlook) can't directly request ARM tokens
because /common blocks them for ARM scopes. The fix is a two-step flow:

1. Auth via /common with openid profile scope (accepts ALL account types)
2. Extract tenant ID from id_token
3. Request ARM token via tenant-specific endpoint using refresh_token

This means personal accounts that have an Azure subscription (which creates
an org directory) can sign in wit

Add day-scoped file cache to avoid repeated logins

Vklass blocks repeated login attempts within the same day. Cache the
full scraper result to /tmp/vklass_cache_YYYY-MM-DD.json on first run;
subsequent calls read from cache instantly without authenticating.
Pass --refresh to force a live scrape and overwrite the cache.

refactor: 重构后端目录结构

- api/: 接口声明层（路由定义，不含具体实现）
- services/: 业务逻辑层（房间服务、用户服务等）
- games/: 游戏逻辑层（狼人杀、阿瓦隆等）
- models/: 数据模型层
- db.py: 数据访问层（纯数据库操作）

分层架构：
- API 层只定义路由和请求/响应模型
- Service 层处理业务逻辑
- Game 层实现具体游戏规则
- DB 层负责数据持久化

优势：
- 职责分离，易于维护
- 业务逻辑可复用
- 游戏逻辑独立，便于扩展

- Fix stale-closure bug in PhaserContainer: connectionManager is now
  stored in a ref so scene-ready callbacks always read the latest value,
  not the mount-time snapshot captured by the [] useEffect closure.

  flag set before emitting 'scene-ready'. PhaserContainer checks this
  flag when registering the listener, so even if the event already fired

fix: increase hooks request timeout from 5s to 15s

Gateway may take a few seconds to accept the hook request before
starting to process — 5s was too tight and caused premature failure
before the 60s polling deadline was reached.

refactor: extract @clawwork/core package with port-based DI (#178)

Move stores, session sync, gateway dispatcher, protocol parsing, and
error classification into a new @clawwork/core package. Desktop stores
become thin re-exports wired through an ElectronPlatformAdapter.

- Port interfaces: PersistencePort, GatewayTransportPort, SettingsPort,
  NotificationsPort
- Factory functions: createMessageStore, createTaskStore, createUiStore,
  createSessionSync, createGatewayDispatcher
- Protocol pure

feat: compile merchant extension source to V8 bytecode in packaged builds

Adds an afterPack step that converts extensions-merchant/*.mjs to V8
bytecode (.jsc) using the packaged Electron binary, replacing original
source with thin loader wrappers. Prevents casual reverse-engineering
of private extension business logic.

- ESM sources are first converted to CJS via esbuild (bundle mode to
  inline workspace deps like @rivonclaw/core)
- Compiled with vm.Script + createCachedData using packaged El

fix: don't split field labels on commas — use JSON array directly

Field labels containing commas (e.g. consent checkbox text) were
split into fragments. Now writes each field as a separate line to
a temp file, reads with sort -u and jq, preserving full labels.

fix: use /organizations endpoint and user_impersonation scope for Azure OAuth

The ARM API requires work/school accounts (Azure AD), not personal Microsoft
accounts. Using /common with .default scope causes 'not enabled for consumers'
errors when users try to sign in with personal accounts (outlook.com, etc).

Changes:
- Auth route: use /organizations endpoint instead of /common
- Auth route: use user_impersonation scope instead of .default
- Auth route: add prompt=select_account for better UX

fix: recognize all available auth statuses for custom providers in WebUI (#1721)

* fix: recognize all available auth statuses for custom providers in WebUI

Custom providers may have auth_status of "not_required" or
"configured_cli" which are valid available states. Previously only
"configured" was recognized, causing custom providers to appear
unconfigured/disabled despite working correctly.

Closes #1711

- runAt: ISO timestamp for one-time schedule
- timezone: default Asia/Taipei
- Validates cron expressions, computes nextRunAt automatically

fix(weixin): register chat metadata before forwarding messages

NanoClaw's messages table has a FOREIGN KEY on chat_jid referencing
chats(jid). When a WeChat message arrives, the chat JID hasn't been
registered yet, causing SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_FOREIGNKEY error.

Send a /metadata request to register the chat before each /message
forward. This mirrors what other NanoClaw channels (WhatsApp, etc.)
do via the onChatMetadata callback.

   - 接收任务 ID 列表（可选）

     - 规则 1：如果 startDate <= 当前时间 且 status = 'todo' → 更新为 'in_progress'

   - 我的任务列表

fix: add Gateway config to openclaw.json — prevent auth failure in microVM (V29)

Without a gateway section in openclaw.json, OpenClaw Gateway may generate
a random auth token. The openclaw agent CLI wouldn't know this token,
causing 'Gateway auth failed' and falling back to embedded mode silently.

Fix: add explicit gateway config with auth.mode=none for local loopback
connections. The microVM gateway is local-only (127.0.0.1:18789), so
no auth is needed. This ensures openclaw agent CLI always

  caused query/document vector space mismatch — cosine similarity dropped

- Fix: embed raw cleaned text only; store context as metadata fields.

Architecture fix: zero invasion to OpenClaw — use Gateway mode as intended.

  → openclaw agent C

Add extended benchmarks: 6 new Ollama models tested against apple-llm

Benchmarked phi3:3.8b, mistral:7b, qwen2.5:7b, gemma2:9b, mistral-nemo:12b,
and phi3:14b across latency, memory, and 6 quality tasks on Mac mini M4 16GB.

Key findings:
- mistral-nemo:12b scored 6/6 on quality (only perfect score)
- gemma2:9b best balance of quality (5/6), speed, and memory
- phi3:14b worst value: slowest, most RAM, only 3/6 quality
- Recommend gemma2:9b as default local fallback for apple-llm

MCP tools (mcp_*) are user-defined external integrations where
read-heavy workflows are expected and legitimate. The read-only
streak detector was designed for filesystem tool loops but caught
MCP tools in the default fallback, triggering false positives on
workflows like "query inbox + read 10 emails + summarize."

Treat mcp_* tools as neutral (same as exec/bash) since GoClaw
cannot determine whether an MCP tool is rea

fix(manager): inject NANOCLAW_NO_CONTAINER as real env var, not just .env

container-runtime.ts reads process.env.NANOCLAW_NO_CONTAINER at module
load time, before NanoClaw's readEnvFile() parses the .env file. The
value was only in NANOCLAW_CONFIG_CONTENT (written to .env by entrypoint)
so process.env never had it, causing the Docker check to run and crash.

Fix dashboard URL in openclaw-list by reading bind from .env

The script read the gateway bind value from openclaw.json via sudo jq,
which could fail silently if jq isn't installed on the host or the data
directory has restrictive permissions (owned by container uid 1000).

Read OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_BIND from the .env file instead — this is the
authoritative source that controls the container's --bind flag, and it
lives in the instance directory which is always accessible.

Make duplicate plugin registration idempotent across retries and stop/reload flows, and add regression coverage for failure rollback and stale stop handling.

Fix missing dashboard URL for instances without openclaw.json

The dashboard URL generation was gated on openclaw.json existing, but the
gateway token lives in .env (always created by openclaw-new) and bind
defaults to loopback. Instances without a config file (not yet onboarded)
would show no dashboard URL at all.

Now generate the dashboard URL whenever api_port is available, reading
bind and token from their respective sources independently.

addColumns creates columns with inconsistent nullability causing Arrow
schema errors on mergeInsert. New approach: old tables skip new fields

seed record (consistent schema). To migrate: rebuild the table fresh.

Fix LanceDB schema nullability mismatch — skip new columns in upsert for addColumns-migrated tables

Root cause: addColumns() creates nullable columns, but direct JS object upsert
infers non-nullable schema. LanceDB's Arrow backend rejects the mismatch.

Fix: Track whether new columns came from seed record (non-nullable, safe) or
addColumns (nullable, skip in upsert). Old tables skip new fields until a full
re-index creates a fresh table with the correct schema.

fix: AnimPreview animation constantly resetting due to unstable offsets reference

The offsets prop was created inline via .map() on every render, causing
the useEffect to re-run and reset frameIdx to 0 each time. This made
animations appear stuck at fps=1 or flash erratically at higher fps.

Fix: store offsets in a ref so the interval is only recreated when
frames or fps actually change. Applied to both CreateCharacterModal
and GifMakerTab. Default fps restored to 4 (matching GifMakerTab).

memory section to be honest about what we can and can't measure,

feat(config): warn on unknown YAML fields at startup to catch typos (#1658)

* feat(config): warn on unknown YAML fields at startup to catch typos

Add startup config validation that logs warnings for unrecognized YAML
fields. Go's yaml.v2 silently ignores unknown fields, causing typos
like "top_k" (should be "topk") to use zero values with no indication.

Implementation:
- Double-parse: normal unmarshal + generic map walk with reflection
- Recursively compares raw YAML keys against struct YAML

fix(security): add defensive validation to tmpdir cleanup in install.sh (#3000)

Adds a non-empty check after mktemp and guards the EXIT trap so rm -rf
only fires when tmpdir is non-empty and still a directory. This is a
defense-in-depth hardening — the current code is safe due to set -e,
but explicit validation is best practice for rm -rf operations.

Fixes #2998

fix: correct Python heredoc argument order for marketplace sync

The Python script was trying to open seed file as a Python script file
instead of reading it as data. Arguments must come BEFORE heredoc marker.

Changed from:
  python3 <<'PYTHON_EOF' "" ""

To:
  python3 "" "" <<'PYTHON_EOF'

This allows sys.argv[1] and sys.argv[2] to correctly receive the file paths
as data arguments, not as script files to execute.

fix: escape backslashes in Python regex for Windows path support

The regex pattern r'marketplaces[/\]([^/\]+)' was not properly
escaping backslashes in the character class. In Python raw strings,
backslash still needs to be doubled inside character classes.

Changed to r'marketplaces[/\\]([^/\\]+)' to correctly match both
forward slash and backslash in marketplace paths.

This fixes plugin path synchronization failing silently on container startup.

feat: seed-based plugin config sync (host → container)

彻底解决插件配置路径问题，采用 seed 同步架构。

**问题根源**:
- known_marketplaces.json 包含宿主机绝对路径
- 无法使用 ~ 相对路径（不被支持）
- 直接 bind mount 会导致宿主机/容器路径冲突

**新架构设计**:
```
宿主机 (绝对路径)                  容器 (独立副本)
~/.claude/plugins/                 /home/node/.claude/plugins/
  known_marketplaces.json  ──────>   known_marketplaces.json
  (bind mount, RO)       seed       (writable, 转换后路径)

  marketplaces/           ──────>   marketplaces-host/  (RO mount)
  (bind mount, RO)

`mirror` object is never constructed, and `appendAssistantMessageToSessionTranscript()`
is never called.

Initial release: Swift CLI bridge for Apple Foundation Models

Lightweight CLI that exposes Apple's on-device Foundation Models (Apple Intelligence)
via stdin/stdout. Designed for scripting and automation — load, infer, exit with zero
idle RAM cost.

Features:
- Stdin pipe and --prompt flag input
- Streaming output (default) and --no-stream buffered mode
- JSON input/output mode for programmatic integration
- System prompt support
- Configurable max tokens and temperature
- Clean error handling

feat: 完成 DDD 架构核心实现

架构分层:
- config/ 配置管理 (zod schema 验证)
- shared/ 共享层 (错误、类型)
- domain/ 领域层 (Request + Task 领域)
  - Entity (富实体，包含业务逻辑)
  - Repository (接口定义)
  - Service (领域服务)
- infrastructure/ 基础设施层
  - SQLite 客户端 + 数据库迁移
  - Repository 实现 (依赖注入)
  - HTTP 路由 (Express)
- app/ 应用层
  - DI 容器 (Inversify)
  - Bootstrap 引导
  - HTTP 服务器

技术栈:
- inversify (依赖注入)
- reflect-metadata (反射元数据)
- zod (Schema 验证)
- express (HTTP 框架)
- node:sqlite (Node 22+)

架构特点:
- 依赖倒置原则
- Repository 模式
- 富实体模型
- 类型安全

fix(entrypoint): use ~ paths for shared plugin config

关键修正：插件配置文件在宿主机和容器间共享，必须使用 ~ 相对路径。

**问题分析**:
- known_marketplaces.json 通过 bind mount 在宿主机和容器间共享
- 如果使用 /home/node 绝对路径：
  - 容器内：✅ 正常展开为 /home/node/.claude/...
  - 宿主机：❌ 无法识别（~ 仍是 /home/node 而非用户主目录）

**正确方案**:
- 使用 ~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/<name> 格式
- 宿主机：~ → /Users/<user>, /home/<user>, C:/Users/<user>
- 容器：~ → /home/node
- 两边都能正确展开！

**修复内容**:
- 正则表达式：marketplaces[/\\]([^/\\]+) 提取插件名
- 转换目标：~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/<name>
- 兼容

fix(entrypoint): robust plugin path normalization for all platforms

彻底解决容器内 Claude Code 插件路径问题，确保所有用户（新/旧、跨平台）都能正常使用。

**问题根源**:
- known_marketplaces.json 从宿主机 bind mount 到容器
- 宿主机路径是绝对路径（/Users/xxx, /home/xxx, C:/Users/xxx）
- 容器内需要 /home/node/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/xxx
- 之前尝试用 ~ 相对路径，但对存量用户无效

**彻底解决方案**:
- entrypoint 启动时自动检测并修复路径
- 使用 Python 正则表达式提取 marketplace 名称
- 支持所有平台：macOS/Linux/Windows
- 完整备份机制，失败自动回滚
- 兼容新用户（可能用 ~）和存量用户（绝对路径）

**修复逻辑**:
1. 检测文件是否存在
2. 检查是否已有容器路径（避免重复修复）
3. 使用正则提

fix: community search finds 0 — saveDeviceData before setEntityPublic (#512)

Root cause: setEntityPublic runs UPDATE on entities table, but the entity
row doesn't exist yet (saveDeviceData hasn't been called for this device).
UPDATE hits 0 rows silently.

Fixes:
1. index.js: reorder — saveDeviceData().then(setEntityPublic()) so entity
   row exists before is_public update
2. db.js: setEntityPublic fallback INSERT ON CONFLICT if UPDATE hits 0 rows
   (belt + suspenders)

Co-authored-by: Mac_Clau
Weekend reminders updated: + organize hillside garage

fix(website): add SPA fallback for GitHub Pages keynote routes (#175)

Direct access to /keynote/<slide> returned 404 because GitHub Pages
has no server-side routing. Added a root 404.html that encodes the
path into a query string and redirects to the correct SPA entry point,
plus a redirect-back script in both website and keynote index.html.

Signed-off-by: samzong <samzong.lu@gmail.com>

fix(pptPreview): return error result instead of throwing in provider callback

The bridge subscribe() function lacks .catch(), so errors thrown from provider
callbacks become unhandled promise rejections (Sentry ELECTRON-CT, 144 events).
Return { url: '', error: message } instead of re-throwing, and check for the
error field in the renderer before using the URL.

Fixes ELECTRON-CT

an isDestroyed guard in the poll callback.

### Bug Fixes

* persist is_public to DB when set via PUT agent-card ([#510](https://github.com/HankHuang0516/EClaw/issues/510)) ([dd8eab4](https://github.com/HankHuang0516/EClaw/commit/dd8eab4a1756d702bc0c83bb9b2748f054fc1928))

fix: persist is_public to DB when set via PUT agent-card (#510)

PUT /api/entity/agent-card with public:true only stored isPublic in memory
(entity.isPublic). The entities table is_public column was never updated
because saveDeviceData doesn't include is_public.

feat: add 5s per-device debounce to mission notify endpoint (#509)

Batch operations (e.g. adding 6 skills at once) now get debounced:
- First call: queued + immediate ACK to caller
- Subsequent calls within 5s: appended to queue + ACK
- After 5s of inactivity: flush all notifications in one consolidated push
- Deduplication: same type+title notifications are merged
- immediate=true flag bypasses debounce (for manual/direct triggers)
- In-memory only, per-device isolation

fix(kb): persist document content in Gateway DB for vector recovery

The knowledge_docs table only stored metadata (name, file_path, size)
but not the original document content. If the gateway pod restarted,
all knowledge files and .memory.db on emptyDir were lost —
knowledge_search would return empty results permanently.

- Add `content` column to knowledge_docs (SQLite + MySQL schemas, DDL,
  and migrations)
- Persist original content on kb.upload
- On gateway startup, restore knowledge files

fix: resolve false "unsaved changes" after auto-save in AgentBuilder canvas

Hash computation was inconsistent between graph load and SaveManager:
- Main load path used stale storeState.graphStateFields/fallbackNodeId (previous graph's values)
- applyLoadedGraph omitted stateFields and fallbackNodeId entirely (defaulted to undefined)
Both caused SaveManager to compute a different hash on next save, triggering hasPendingChanges=true immediately after load.

Fix: parse stateFields and fallbackNode

Fix conversation history loading — use getRecentMessages() not sessionId filter

ConversationHistoryArbiter.addMessage stores messages under a backend-generated
UUID (this.currentSessionId), not the frontend's soma_session_id. The sessionId
from the request goes into metadata only. getRecentMessages() with no filter
returns the active session correctly; the sessionId query param was always ignored.

- /api/soma/history: removed broken sessionId filter, uses getRecentMessages(limit)
- /api/orb/hi

Performance: Groups API pagination + ลด polling interval 5s → 15s

- Groups API เพิ่ม pagination (limit/page params, default 50)
- Response format เปลี่ยนเป็น { groups, pagination } พร้อม metadata
- ทุก consumer รองรับทั้ง format เก่า (array) และใหม่ (object)
- Conversation list polling ลดจาก 5s เป็น 15s (inbox + chat)
- Message polling ยังคง 5s เหมือนเดิม

fix: delegate ALL cloud credentials, not just the current cloud (#2994)

delegateCloudCredentials only copied the current cloud's config file
(e.g. sprite.json when spawning on Sprite). Child VMs couldn't spawn
on other clouds because their tokens weren't forwarded.

Now iterates all known clouds and copies every credential file that
exists locally, so the agent can spawn children on any cloud.

safety net against any future unhandled exceptions f

reasoning_callback, causing duplicate display.

vs <think> tag extraction. Only fire the callback for <think>-extracted

fix: preserve RunProfile selection across session tab switches

The useEffect that reset selectedRunProfileId on every activeKey change
was clearing the UI dropdown and pushing null to the resolver, wiping
valid profile data. The resolver already retains per-session profiles
correctly — the bug was purely in the UI layer.

Cache selectedRunProfileId in SessionChatState so tab switches restore
the dropdown, and remove the blanket-reset useEffect entirely.

defaults['model']. The CLI reads them from CLI_CONFIG['model']['provider']
so root-level keys were silently ignored, causing fallback to OpenRouter.

Merge root-level provider and base_url into defaults['model'] after
handling the model key, so custom/local provider configs

fix(acp): cap keepalive resets to promptOriginTime wall-clock budget

A process that hangs (alive but making no progress) would cause the
keepalive to reset timeouts indefinitely. Fix by tracking promptOriginTime
on each PendingRequest (set at creation, never updated), and only resetting
the timeout from the keepalive when Date.now() - promptOriginTime is still
within the request's timeoutDuration budget.

This means a hung process will time out after at most one full timeout
window beyond the p

self.base_url, self.api_mode, and self._client_kwargs were all updated
but self.api_key was not. delegate_tool.py reads parent_agent.api_key
to pass credentials to child agents, so subagents inherited the stale
pre-fallback key (e.g. a minimax key sent to OpenRouter), causing 401
Missing Authentication errors.

fix: use sys.executable for pip in update commands to fix PEP 668 (#3099)

The update commands called bare 'pip' as fallback when uv wasn't found.
On modern Debian/Ubuntu enforcing PEP 668, this resolves to system pip
which refuses to install in an externally-managed environment.

Use sys.executable -m pip to ensure the venv's pip is used. Fixed in
both cmd_update and _update_via_zip (the PR only caught one instance).

fix: installSpawnCli fails on Sprite — bun shim doesn't work (#2993)

Sprite has a bun shim at /.sprite/bin/bun that delegates to
$HOME/.bun/bin/bun, but that binary doesn't exist on fresh VMs.
`command -v bun` returns true (finds the shim) so the install script
skips bun installation, then bun fails when actually invoked.

Fixed in two places:
- installSpawnCli: source shell profiles, test `bun --version` (not
  just existence), and install bun fresh if it doesn't work
- install.sh: replace `co
docs: add WomensMagazine stale publish incident report

fix(acp): use isChildAlive() to guard keepalive against naturally-exited child

ChildProcess.killed is only set to true when the process is terminated
via Node's .kill() method. A child that crashes naturally (exit code 1,
OOM, etc.) leaves killed=false until the exit event is processed by the
JS event loop.

In that narrow window the keepalive interval could fire, see killed===false,
and call resetSessionPromptTimeouts() on a process that has already died.

Fix: extract a private isChildAlive()

fix(cli): prevent update crash in non-TTY environments (#3094)

cmd_update calls input() unconditionally during config migration.
In headless environments (Telegram gateway, systemd), there's no TTY,
so input() throws EOFError and the update crashes.

Guard with sys.stdin.isatty(), default to skipping the migration
prompt when non-interactive.

fix(agent): prevent AsyncOpenAI/httpx cross-loop deadlock in gateway mode (#2701)

In gateway mode, async tools (vision_analyze, web_extract, session_search)
deadlock because _run_async() spawns a thread with asyncio.run(), creating
a new event loop, but _get_cached_client() returns an AsyncOpenAI client
bound to a different loop. httpx.AsyncClient cannot work across event loop
boundaries, causing await client.chat.completions.create() to hang forever.

values restored by streaming deobfuscation). The fetch intercept was
re-obfuscating them, creating a second fake that the LLM echoed back
alongside the first — producing two JSON blocks in the response.

user messages, tool results, and the system prompt.

fix: rename API timeout — respond before DB save, add push timeout guard (#494)

Root cause: PUT /api/device/entity/name called await saveData() which saves
ALL devices to PostgreSQL (expensive), blocking the HTTP response for >10s.

Fixes:
1. Send res.json() BEFORE persisting — rename response is now instant
2. Use db.saveDeviceData() (single device) instead of saveData() (all devices)
3. DB save runs in background (fire-and-forget with error logging)
4. Channel push wrapped in 5s Promise.race

fix(agent): count compression restarts toward retry limit (#3070)

When context overflow triggers compression, the outer retry loop
restarts via continue without incrementing retry_count. If compression
reduces messages but not enough to fit the context window, this creates
an infinite loop burning API credits: API call → overflow → compress →
retry → overflow → compress → ...

Increment retry_count on compression restarts so the loop exits after
max_retries total attempts.

Description
This PR fixes the startup crash introduced in v0.4.0 where `self.base_url` being `None` throws a `TypeError`.

Root Cause:
At `cli.py:1108`, a membership check (`"openrouter.ai" in self.base_url`) is performed. If a user's config doesn't explicitly set a `base_url` (meaning it's `None`), Python raises a `TypeError: argument of type 'NoneType' is not iterable`, causing the entire CLI to crash on boot.

causing race conditions when multiple platforms (Telegram + Discord)
received messages concurrently on the same gateway process. Two threads
could simultaneously pass the session_key check and create duplicate
sessions for the same user, splitting conversation history.

fix(display): guard isatty() against closed streams via _is_tty property (#3056)

In gateway/Telegram mode, the stdout fd can be closed by executor
thread cleanup. KawaiiSpinner.stop() called isatty() on the closed fd,
raising ValueError and masking the original error.

Instead of a point fix, add a _is_tty property that centralizes the
closed-stream guard — both _animate() and stop() now use it. Follows
the same (ValueError, OSError) pattern already in _write().

on the same row as the spinner, causing visual overlap.

printing background task output, ensuring the TUI layout is in a consistent
state before the output is written.

feat: Phase 16 — tiered memory with exponential decay (short/medium/long)

Three-tier memory system with Grok's decay model:
- TieredLesson dataclass with score, last_reinforced, sessions_validated
- Decay: score *= 0.85/day; reinforce: score = min(1.0, score + 0.3)
- Auto-promote at score >= 0.9 AND sessions >= 3; GC at score < 0.2
- Short-tier: in-process only (short_set/get/clear/all), no file I/O
- Medium-tier: memory/medium/lessons.jsonl — decays, promotes, GC
- Long-tier: memory/long/lesso

fix(cli): catch KeyboardInterrupt during flush_memories on exit (#3025)

KeyboardInterrupt inherits from BaseException, not Exception, so the
except Exception: clauses wrapping flush_memories() on exit paths
silently skipped the flush when the user pressed Ctrl+C. This could
lose conversation memory.

Change both call sites to except (Exception, KeyboardInterrupt): so
the memory flush is attempted even during interrupt.

Salvaged from PR #2855 by RufusLin (dropped unrelated bundled changes).

fix(gateway): disable Qwen3 thinking mode for professional responses

Qwen3-32B has built-in chain-of-thought reasoning that consumes output
tokens silently. With include_reasoning:false, all 1024 tokens go to
the actual response. Latency drops from ~13s to ~10s.

A/B test vs DeepSeek V3.1 showed Qwen3 quality is equivalent for
PT-BR HVAC-R professional content at 3.5x lower latency.

fix(agent): restore safe non-streaming fallback after stream failures (#3020)

After streaming retries are exhausted on transient errors, fall back to
non-streaming instead of propagating the error. Also fall back for any
other pre-delivery stream error (not just 'streaming not supported').

Added user-facing message when streaming is not supported by a model/
provider, directing users to set display.streaming: false in config.yaml
to avoid the fallback delay.

fix: status bar shows 26K instead of 260K for token counts with trailing zeros (#3024)

format_token_count_compact() used unconditional rstrip("0") to clean up
decimal trailing zeros (e.g. "1.50" → "1.5"), but this also stripped
meaningful trailing zeros from whole numbers ("260" → "26", "100" → "1").
Guard the strip behind a decimal-point check.

fix(delegate): give subagents independent iteration budgets (#3004)

Each subagent now gets its own IterationBudget instead of sharing the
parent's.  The per-subagent cap is controlled by delegation.max_iterations
in config.yaml (default 50).  Total iterations across parent + subagents
can exceed the parent's max_iterations, but the user retains control via
the config setting.

Previously, subagents shared the parent's budget, so three parallel
subagents configured for max_iterations=50 racing a
onboard

fix: don't follow redirects server-side in MITM proxy (#108)

* fix: don't follow redirects server-side in MITM proxy

reqwest's default redirect policy follows 3xx responses internally,
collapsing the redirect chain. Clients that depend on headers from
intermediate responses (e.g. huggingface_hub reading X-Repo-Commit
from a 302) break because they only see the final 200.

Disable redirect following so 3xx responses are forwarded as-is.

fix(cron): mark session as ended after job completes (#2998)

Cron was the only execution path that never called end_session(),
leaving ended_at = NULL permanently. This made cron sessions invisible
to hermes prune --older-than and indistinguishable from active sessions.

Captures session_id in a local variable before agent construction so
it's available in the finally block even if AIAgent() fails, then calls
end_session(session_id, 'cron_complete') before close().

or silently swallowed, causing sessions to never be indexed in the FTS5
database. This made session_search unable to find affected conversations.

   - Mock(return_value=(0, 0, [])) → lambda: (0, 0, [])
   - Mock(return_value=True/False) → lambda *args, **kwargs: True/False
   - Complex Mock classes → simple Python classes with __init__

   - Mock(return_value=result) → lamb

plugin-runtime: expose runHeartbeatOnce in system API (#40299)

* plugin-runtime: expose runHeartbeatOnce in system API

Plugins that enqueue system events and need the agent to deliver
responses to the originating channel currently have no way to
override the default `heartbeat.target: "none"` behaviour.

Expose `runHeartbeatOnce` in the plugin runtime `system` namespace
so plugins can trigger a single heartbeat cycle with an explicit
`heartbeat: { target: "last" }` override — the same pattern

fix(display): suppress KawaiiSpinner animation under patch_stdout (#2994)

When the CLI is active, sys.stdout is prompt_toolkit's StdoutProxy which
queues writes and injects newlines around each flush(). This causes every
\r spinner frame to land on its own line instead of overwriting the
previous one, producing visible flickering where the spinner and status
bar repeatedly swap positions.

The CLI already renders spinner state via a dedicated TUI widget
(_spinner_text / get_spinner_text), so Ka

windowMenu) leave accelerator=null and rely on native macOS fallback
which broke on macOS 16. Additionally fileMenu was never included so
Cmd+W had no menu item at all. Build the app menu explicitly with
Command+H, Command+Q, Command+W, Command+M, and Command+Alt+H.

test(acp): fix timeout test to account for keepalive timer reset

The keepalive fires every 60 s and resets pending prompt timers while
the child process is alive. The existing test configured a 60 s timeout
then advanced timers to 61 s, but the keepalive reset the timer at
exactly 60 s — so cancelPrompt was never called.

Fix: mark child.killed = true at 59 s so the keepalive skips the reset,
letting the original 60 s timeout fire as expected.

fix(plugin): use camelCase for Tauri invoke parameters in Google Calendar panel

Tauri automatically converts snake_case Rust parameters to camelCase in
JavaScript. The panel.js was using snake_case (scheduled_start_at) when
calling create_task and update_task, causing datetime fields to be ignored.

Changed to camelCase (scheduledStartAt, scheduledEndAt, etc.) to match
Tauri parameter name conversion.
